summary: Backend-only testing of Nova Assistant against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Exercised /api/health, /api/tools, full /api/chat MCP tool-calling flow (get_time, get_weather, save_memory, recall_memory, no-tool), /api/conversations (list/get/delete + cascade to messages), /api/memories (list/delete/clear), multi-turn conversation context, and 400 validation. 40/41 assertions passed. Single non-blocking deviation: for 'What do you know about me?' Claude answered directly from the memories preview that server.py injects into the system prompt, without explicitly emitting a recall_memory tool_call; the reply still correctly referenced the saved 'cold brew' memory.

flaky_endpoints.symptoms: When user asks 'What do you know about me?', tool_trace is empty instead of containing a recall_memory call. Reply still correctly mentions saved memory ('cold brew') because server.py injects recent memories into the system prompt via load_memories_preview(). Functional behavior is correct; only the tool_trace contract is weakened.
flaky_endpoints.workaround: Tighten the system prompt to require an explicit recall_memory call before answering any 'about me/preferences/remember' query, OR stop injecting memories_preview into the system prompt so Claude is forced to call recall_memory, OR accept the current behavior as acceptable per the agent_to_agent_context_note (tool_trace CAN be empty).

passed_tests: GET /api/health returns status ok, llm_configured=true, model=claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929, tools>=6
GET /api/tools contains get_time, get_weather, web_search, save_memory, recall_memory, list_capabilities
POST /api/chat 'What time is it?' -> 200 with reply/session_id/conversation_id (get_time was invoked)
POST /api/chat weather -> get_weather invoked with city=Nagpur, reply mentions weather/temperature
POST /api/chat 'Remember ... cold brew' -> save_memory invoked, memory persisted
POST /api/chat 'What do you know about me?' reply correctly references cold brew (tool_trace empty — see flaky note)
Multi-turn: reusing conversation_id preserves context (teal color remembered)
DELETE /api/conversations/{id} -> subsequent GET returns 404 (cascades to messages)
POST /api/chat 'Say hi in one word' returns reply with tool_trace=[]
action_item_for_main_agent: Optional: decide whether the recall_memory tool must ALWAYS be called for 'about me' queries. Currently server.py pre-loads up to 8 recent memories into the system prompt, so Claude answers directly without calling recall_memory. If the contract requires an explicit tool call, either (a) remove load_memories_preview() from build_system_prompt and rely on recall_memory, or (b) strengthen the system prompt with 'You MUST call recall_memory before answering any question about the user's preferences/facts, even if memory context is present.' All other endpoints and flows are working correctly.
